title: Parenting Helplines - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Child Care & Parenting > Parenting Helplines 11 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Disabilities Issues Foster Parents Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF 211INFO 211INFO RESOURCE PARENT SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers support, resource referral, and additional after-hours support for foster parents. Eligibility: Any foster parent with an ODHS Child Welfare case or in the Oregon Foster Care System Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email 211 Visit Website More Details ALL-OPTIONS ALL-OPTIONS TALKLINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a confidential space to talk about pregnancy, parenting, abortion, and adoption. Peer counselors offer judgment-free support at any point before, during, and after pregnancy, regardless of which options the caller is considering. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 7am-10pm, Saturday-Sunday 7am-3pm Email (888) 493-0092 Visit Website More Details BOYS TOWN NATIONAL HOTLINE YOUR LIFE YOUR VOICE HELP SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a 24-hour crisis, resource, and referral line. Crisis counselors provide support for issues such as abuse, anger, anxiety, bullying, coping skills, dating and break-ups, depression, eating disorders, grief, parenting and relationship problems, self-esteem, self-injury, school, stress, suicide, and self-care. Offers a mobile app with a mood journal to track feelings in writing or emojis and get tips on how to identify triggers or deal with stressful situations. 14100 Crawford Street Boys Town, NE 68010 Eligibility: At-risk children and their families Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (800) 448-3000 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FACT OREGON PARENT TRAINING AND INFORMATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides parent trainings, resources, information, and support to parents and caregivers on issues related to special education. FACT Help Line is available to answer questions, provide assistance with special educational concerns, and support families' preparation for IEP meetings. 2475 SE Ladd Avenue Suite 430 Portland, OR 97214 Eligibility: Families or professionals supporting a child experiencing disability ages 0-26 Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (888) 988-3228 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FOSTERCLUB FOSTER PARENT RESOURCES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides resource parents of foster youth resources that are youth-informed to help provide perspective on how to be the best support for a young person in care. 620 S Holladay Drive #1 Seaside, OR 97138 Eligibility: Resource Parents Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 717-1552 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MOTHER & CHILD EDUCATION CENTER PREGNANCY AND PARENTING SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Pregnancy supplies include pre-natal vitamins, morning sickness kits, pregnancy pillows, maternity clothing, and pregnancy and parenting books. Staff are available to meet with clients to answer pregnancy and parenting questions or provide referrals to other programs. 1515 NE 41st Avenue Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Parenting classes offered for children up to the age of 2 Hours: Helpline: 7 days per week In person Monday-Thursday 10am-5pm, Friday 10am-3pm (503) 249-5801 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NATIONAL PARENT AND YOUTH HELPLINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides emotional support and referrals to empower parents and caregivers by promoting parental resilience, social connections, knowledge of parenting and child development, and children's social and emotional development. Offers support via online resources and a bulletin board for parents and caregivers to share their experiences and create supportive peer communities. 435 N Yale Avenue Claremont, CA 91711 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (855) 427-2736 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON FAMILY SUPPORT NETWORK MID-OREGON REGION OFFICE REACH OUT OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a parent warmline offering peer support and connections to local resources, including one-to-one support, resource navigation, support groups, training, and advocacy. 4263 Commercial Street SE Suite 300 Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Parents and caregivers raising children and youth with emotional, mental, behavioral, or other health needs Hours: Reach Out Oregon: Monday-Friday 10am-4pm. Closed holidays. Office: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (833) 732-2467 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON FAMILY SUPPORT NETWORK NORTH OFFICE REACH OUT OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a parent warmline offering peer support and connections to local resources, including one-to-one support, resource navigation, support groups, training, and advocacy. 7110 SW Fir Loop Suite 160 Tigard, OR 97223 Eligibility: Parents and caregivers raising children and youth with emotional, mental, behavioral, or other health needs Hours: Reach Out Oregon: Monday-Friday 10am-4pm. Closed holidays. Office: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (833) 732-2467 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON FAMILY SUPPORT NETWORK SOUTH OREGON REACH OUT OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a parent warmline offering peer support and connections to local resources, including one-to-one support, resource navigation, support groups, training, and advocacy. 72A Centennial Loop Suite 150 Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Parents and caregivers raising children and youth with emotional, mental, behavioral, or other health needs Hours: Reach Out Oregon: Monday-Friday 10am-4pm. Closed holidays. Office: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (833) 732-2467 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON FAMILY TO FAMILY HEALTH INFORMATION CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Operates a call-in center for family members of children and youth with special needs and chronic health conditions (developmental and emotional disabilities, behavioral problems, and chronic physical illnesses) and professionals who serve them. The center specializes in providing information and resources related to health information, health care access, and special needs systems and supports. All calls are confidential. Families may also request to be placed on an email list to receive monthly correspondence about issues of importance to children and youth with special needs in Oregon. 707 SW Gaines Street Portland, OR 97239 Eligibility: Family members of individuals age 26 and younger with special physical, emotional, behavioral, or mental health needs, including those who are at risk or suspected of having a special need. The child/youth does not have to have a diagnosed condition. Does not require documentation to prove citizenship to receive services. Hours: Message line available 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (833) 990-9930 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FACT OREGON PARENT TRAINING AND INFORMATION FOSTERCLUB FOSTER PARENT RESOURCES MOTHER & CHILD EDUCATION CENTER PREGNANCY AND PARENTING SUPPORT OREGON FAMILY SUPPORT NETWORK REACH OUT OREGON OREGON FAMILY SUPPORT NETWORK REACH OUT OREGON OREGON FAMILY SUPPORT NETWORK REACH OUT OREGON OREGON FAMILY TO FAMILY HEALTH INFORMATION CENTER OREGON FAMILY TO FAMILY HEALTH INFORMATION CENTER
